provenance.source_corpus: VEGETATION RESPONSES TO ECOLOGICAL RESTORATION (REWETTING) OF ABANDONED BLOCK-CUT PEATLANDS IN EASTERN QUÉBEC

source_spans.text: Ecological restoration of abandoned blockucut peatlands involves blocking remnant drainage ditches, “rewetting” the residual peat body
rapid community scale vegetation change occurred
Sphagnum cover increased in all rewetted sectors
The rise in water table position due to rewetting caused large-scale tree mortality and a reduction in living tree biomass
Fibric peat accumulation is an order of magnitude higher for all rewetted trenches than in nonrewetted trenches

edges.subject: causal_mosaic:node:6598cd87e501
edges.original_sentence: rewetting activities within the three study sites has variably, but successfully forced a shift in vegetation assemblages
